# Lys

> Zero-dependency CSS + TS slide engine. Turns `<article>` elements into accessible, navigable presentations. Visually unopinionated, behaviorally opinionated. Designed for LLM-generated single-file HTML.

## HTML Contract

Slides are `<article>` elements inside a `<div data-lys>` container. Use standard HTML elements inside each article.

### Data Attributes (on `<article>`)

| Attribute | Purpose | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `data-notes` | Speaker notes (text) | `data-notes="Mention Q3 figures"` |
| `data-transition` | Transition mode (`"fade"` enables fade for the deck) | `data-transition="fade"` |
| `data-class` | CSS classes added to the slide | `data-class="title-slide dark"` |
| `data-background` | Background shorthand (color, gradient, URL) | `data-background="#1a1a2e"` |
| `data-timing` | Suggested duration in seconds | `data-timing="30"` |

### Chapter Nav (opt-in)

Add a nav region with `data-lys-nav` *inside* the `<div data-lys>`, with `<a href="#id">` links pointing at slide `id`s. You write the links and all styling; Lys positions the region out of the scroll-snap flow and marks the current slide's link with `aria-current="true"` and `data-lys-nav-active` (style this for the "you are here" state). A slide is in the nav if it has an `id` â€” no new attribute. Links scroll to slides even without JS.

```html
<div data-lys>
  <nav data-lys-nav aria-label="Slides">
    <a href="#intro">Intro</a>
    <a href="#results">Results</a>
  </nav>
  <article id="intro"><h1>Intro</h1></article>
  <article id="results"><h2>Results</h2></article>
</div>
```

## CSS Tokens

Set `--lys-*` properties on `:root`, `[data-lys]`, or individual `<article>` elements to customize.

| Token | Default |
|---|---|
| `--lys-aspect-ratio` | `16 / 9` |
| `--lys-slide-padding` | `4cqi` |
| `--lys-transition-duration` | `300ms` |
| `--lys-transition-easing` | `ease-in-out` |
| `--lys-font-size-base` | `clamp(0.75rem, 2.5cqi, 1.5rem)` |
| `--lys-slide-gap` | `0` |
| `--lys-slide-max-width` | `100vw` |
| `--lys-slide-max-height` | `100vh` |
| `--lys-focus-ring` | `2px solid currentColor` |
| `--lys-backdrop` | `light-dark(#fff, #000)` |

## Events

Emitted on the `[data-lys]` container element, with `bubbles: true`.

- **`lys:ready`** â€” `{ detail: { instance } }` â€” Initialization complete. The instance exposes the JS API below.
- **`lys:slidechange`** â€” `{ detail: { from: number, to: number, slide: HTMLElement } }` â€” Slide changed.

## JS API

```
Lys.init()           â†’ Lys[]          // Auto-discover all [data-lys] containers
Lys.from(el)         â†’ Lys            // Get or create instance (idempotent)
new Lys(el)          â†’ Lys            // Create instance for a container

instance.next()                       // Next slide (no-op at end)
instance.prev()                       // Previous slide (no-op at start)
instance.goTo(index | id)             // Jump by 0-based index or article id
instance.destroy()                    // Remove listeners, revert state

instance.current     â†’ number         // 0-based current slide index (-1 if empty)
instance.total       â†’ number         // Total slide count
instance.slide       â†’ HTMLElement|null // Current slide element
```

Navigation: Arrow keys, Space/Shift+Space, Home/End, touch swipe, hash routing (`#slide=3` or `#slide=intro`).

IIFE build auto-initializes on DOMContentLoaded. ESM build exposes `Lys` for manual control.

## Inline Example

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>My Deck</title>
  <style>/* contents of lys.css */</style>
  <style>
    :root {
      --lys-slide-padding: 3rem 4rem;
      --lys-aspect-ratio: 16/9;
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <div data-lys>
    <article data-notes="Opening slide">
      <h1>Title</h1>
      <p>Subtitle text here.</p>
    </article>
    <article data-background="#1a1a2e" data-notes="Main content">
      <h2>Second Slide</h2>
      <ul>
        <li>Point one</li>
        <li>Point two</li>
      </ul>
    </article>
  </div>
  <script>/* contents of lys.iife.js */</script>
</body>
</html>
```

## File Sizes

| File | Raw | Gzip |
|---|---|---|
| `lys.css` | 2.9 KB | 1.0 KB |
| `lys.iife.js` | 6.8 KB | 2.5 KB |
<!-- /LYS:SIZES -->
